name: The Independent Visual Consultant
summary: A professional freelance designer or studio owner who manages multiple client accounts. They are acutely aware of their time value and require tools that offer extreme customization, brand fidelity, and seamless client presentation. Efficiency and perceived value are their primary drivers.
goals: Establish and maintain robust brand consistency across all client projects.
Drastically minimize the time spent on repetitive setup tasks (e.g., manually building new client brand kits).
Deliver polished, non-editable client presentation views that minimize training time for end-users.
Capture maximum value from their time, ensuring software processes directly contribute to billable hours.
pains: The current tool complexity confuses non-technical clients, leading to support overhead.
Workflows requiring extensive manual asset preparation or migration waste billable time.
Limited customization (e.g., inability to brand the client view, forcing the client to use the vendor's brand).
Software pricing models (like per-seat) do not align with multi-client, single-operator freelance structures.
motivations: Monetizing efficiency: Finding features that directly save time and, therefore, increase hourly profitability.
Professionalism: Presenting a unified, highly customized brand experience that reflects their high standards.
Scalability: Operating systems that can handle multiple independent client brands under a single structure.
objections: Tools that mandate a per-seat subscription model when the user is a single proprietor with many clients.
Lack of white-labeling capabilities, which compromises the client's perceived ownership of the final product.
Overly complex interfaces that require significant time investment in learning and training.
decision_triggers: A clear calculation of ROI based on time saved per project.
A demo showcasing white-label capability and client-side branding.
Proof of concept demonstrating smooth asset handoff and export.
sample_quotes: I bill clients hourly so anything that saves me 10 minutes per project is worth real money.
My clients are non-technical. They get confused by your edit interface. Is there a presentation mode?
Love the templates. Saves me hours when starting a new client brand.

journey_stages.mindset: Evaluating specific functionality gaps in competitor tools to justify a higher investment.
key_actions: Comparing client-facing views and customization options.
Investigating enterprise or single-user pricing models.

journey_stages.mindset: Confirming that the solution will save time and elevate professional status.
key_actions: Testing template efficiency with mock client data.
Seeking case studies from high-volume freelancers.
content_preferences: Client testimonial focused on time savings

source_evidence.claim: Wants to maximize efficiency and profit, directly equating time saved to increased revenue.
Values tools that allow high degrees of customization and maintain brand consistency across diverse clients.
Experiences workflow friction from tool complexity when presenting to non-technical end-users.
Models demand for specialized features like white-label client views.
Is sensitive to inflexible pricing models like per-seat licensing, preferring volume or usage-based structures.
Frequently engages in brand definition activities, such as color picking, templating, and asset structuring.
Identifies high technical and creative proficiency roles (Designer, Strategist) working independently.
